events.label: reasoning
events.text: The scenario: backing near a trench without a spotter. OSHA 1926 Subpart O covers motor vehicles, mechanized equipment, and marine operations. Specifically 1926.601(b)(4) says no employer shall permit any construction vehicle to back up unless the vehicle has a reverse signal alarm or an employee signals that it is safe to do so. Also 1926.602(l) covers earthmoving equipment. Backing near a trench is a serious hazard. No spotter = non-compliant. Decision: fail. Risk: high or critical. Backing near a trench without spotter is high risk, possibly critical. I'll say high. Component conditions: non-compliant. Work stage: in-progress. Percent complete: maybe 50? Not specified. I'll estimate 50. Value 0. Sound source empty string. Confidence high ~0.9. Event_time 0, rate 0, order empty, workable empty. Findings: "No spotter used while equipment backing near open trench." Actions: "Stop backing operations; assign a trained spotter or ensure functioning reverse alarm and maintain safe distance from trench edge per OSHA 1926.601/602." References: OSHA 1926.601(b)(4), 1926.602(l).
